channel.title: Mcus on SoundsBrown
channel.description: Recent content in Mcus on SoundsBrown
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Generating audio
item.description: Implementing a VCO using an Arduino is apparently trickier than one might expect. Using a DAC Neither I2C (e.g. MCP4725) nor SPI (e.g. MCP4922) are fast enough to generate audio-rate waveforms. Do not expect to achieve more than, say, 200 Hz. I2S I have thusfar been unable to produce any output with the I2S DAC I have and an MKRZERO. Perhaps try omitting the DAC and verify whether the I2S pins produce any signal at all.

item.title: Burning bootloaders
item.description: In Pololu config utility: Enable VCC output Set regulator mode to 5V Insert cable the right way around: In Arduino IDE: Board: Arduino Uno Programmer: Atmel STK500 Always use “Upload using programmer” Use first of two COM ports (e.g. COM9 if it advertises 9 and 10) Burning bootloader also just works

item.title: STM32
item.description: PlatformIO https://docs.platformio.org/en/latest/boards/ststm32/blackpill_f103c8.html pio init -b blackpill_f103c8 --ide vscode Jumpers on board are set to 0 (closest to the USB port) and 1. This platformio.ini file works: [env:blackpill_f103c8] platform = ststm32 board = genericSTM32F103CB framework = arduino board_build.core = maple upload_protocol = dfu upload_port = COM1 board_build.mcu = stm32f103c8t6 board_build.f_cpu = 72000000L When you get the message Searching for DFU device [1EAF:0003]... during upload, press the reset button on the board.

item.title: Arduino
item.description: Arduino Nano pinout https://store.arduino.cc/arduino-nano Arduino MKR Zero pinout https://store.arduino.cc/arduino-mkr-zero-i2s-bus-sd-for-sound-music-digital-audio-data
